pages.full_text: are called to worship God, not to understand God. No boys in the choir - girls + men. Small but very good singing. Marvellous arch decorations. Misercords. Shrine of St. David's. Simon bought a green knapsack.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a glued-in colour photograph (postcard reference E129) showing Wogan Cavern, Pembroke Castle. The handwritten text below is in pink/magenta ink and continues mid-sentence from the previous spread — it describes a church visit (St. David's Cathedral is implied by 'Shrine of St. David's'). 'Misercords' is the diary's spelling (standard: misericords). No handwritten page number visible at top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
After church the sun shone + we have had continuous hot sunshine all afternoon. We sunbathed, then walked the coastal path. We saw the life boat. An 18 year old became the youngest lifeboat captain after rescuing 5 men. We walked past the golf course to see Joan [?Tillett]'s campsite. The icecreams were delicious. Simon had coffee. I had toffee. No sheep, so Tonic has been off the lead. Lovely wild flowers.
	Simon had a shower. We ate in the van. Rain in the night.
	__Monday May 27__
	It remained overcast until 4 pm. We drove to __Solva__ + parked on the quay. There were young people learning to canoe. The village is pretty but a bit too pretty. A footbridge was opened a hundred years ago by the M.P.
pages.notes: Text is in pink/magenta ink. 'Monday May 27' is underlined and serves as a date heading. 'Solva' is underlined in the text. The surname after 'Joan' is uncertain — could read '[?Tillett]' or '[?Tillets]'; the handwriting is unclear. 'Tonic' appears to be the family dog's name. The year cannot be confirmed from this page alone: May 27 fell on a Monday in 1991, 1996, and 2002, all within the volume's range (1989–2003). The Pembrokeshire context (Pembroke Castle, St. David's, Solva, coastal path) is consistent across those years. No handwritten page number visible at top-right corner. Text ends mid-page; continues on the next spread.
